prose:
Frontier orbital analysis of benzene (C₆H₆, 12 atoms) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), PySCF RKS backend, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied .xyz file (single-point; no re-optimization at this level). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported by the engine. Integrity status: trustworthy = true, all checks passed.

Key results:
- HOMO energy: −7.0468329 eV (index 20)
- LUMO energy: −0.41574728 eV (index 21)
- HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (Koopmans orbital eigenvalue difference, not an excitation energy)

The HOMO and HOMO−1 are essentially degenerate (−7.0468 vs −7.0473 eV), consistent with benzene's e₁g π symmetry; likewise the LUMO and LUMO+1 are near-degenerate (−0.4157 vs −0.4151 eV, e₂u symmetry).

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out ...

No experimental comparison is offered — ask if you want one and a verified source will be located first.
